questionText: Explain continuous integration and continuous deployment.
answerText: CI: automatically test code on every commit. CD: automatically deploy tested code to production. Benefits: faster feedback, reduced manual errors, frequent releases. Tools: GitHub Actions, Jenkins, CircleCI.

questionText: What are Docker containers and why are they useful?
answerText: Containers package application with dependencies in isolated environment. Lightweight compared to VMs. Enable consistent deployment across environments. Facilitate microservices architecture. Use Docker for containerization.

questionText: Explain Kubernetes and its main components.
answerText: Kubernetes: container orchestration platform. Components: Pod (smallest unit), Node (worker machine), Cluster (collection of nodes), Service (network abstraction). Handles deployment, scaling, networking. Steep learning curve but powerful.

Define infrastructure through code/configuration files (Terraform, CloudFormation). Benefits: version control, reproducibility, automation, documentation. Version infrastructure like application code.

questionText: Explain monitoring and logging in DevOps.
answerText: Monitoring: track system metrics (CPU, memory, response time). Logging: record application/system events. Together enable quick issue identification and debugging. Tools: Prometheus, ELK stack, DataDog.
